note: Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/RootSystem/CartanMatrix.lean:120 — the Cartan matrix of a crystallographic root pairing encodes the same combinatorial data as its Dynkin diagram (file explicitly discusses 'connectedness of the Dynkin diagram' at line 228-229), but Mathlib formalizes the matrix, not a diagram/graph object, so this is a nearby encoding rather than the concept itself.
verify_note: decl confirmed; cartanMatrix def is actually at line 108 (not 120, minor drift) and 'connectedness of the Dynkin diagram' docstring is at line 228 as claimed; Wikidata Q5319218 is subclass-of Graph while cartanMatrix is a Matrix type, so 'related' (not 'exact') is the honest, appropriately-hedged call.

note: Mathlib/Analysis/LocallyConvex/Montel.lean:41 — class MontelSpace (Heine-Borel property: closed+bounded implies compact) generalizes the property that classical Montel's theorem establishes for spaces of holomorphic functions; Mathlib proves the abstract TVS class but has no instance/theorem that spaces of holomorphic functions satisfy it (no 'normal family' result found), so this is the generalized structure, not the classical theorem itself.
verify_note: decl confirmed (docstring/class start lines 41-46, minor drift from cited 41); repo-wide grep for 'normal family'/holomorphic in mathlib4 confirms no classical-theorem instance exists, matching the note's claim; Wikidata Q535366 is a theorem named after Montel, and MontelSpace is the abstract generalization of its conclusion — 'generalization' (not 'exact') is honest and matches BRAIN schema's defined match_kind vocabulary.
